content.explanation: DHCP (Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol) automatically hands out IP addresses and other network settings (subnet mask, default gateway, DNS servers) to devices when they join a network, so nobody has to configure every laptop and phone by hand.
content.example:
A laptop joins the office Wi-Fi and broadcasts 'DHCPDISCOVER — does anyone have an IP for me?'. A DHCP server replies with an offer, e.g. 10.20.1.55/24, gateway 10.20.1.1, DNS 10.20.1.10. The laptop accepts it and starts using that address, on lease for a limited time before it must renew.

A Cisco IOS router configured as that DHCP server would define the pool like this:
ip dhcp pool OFFICE-WIFI
 network 10.20.1.0 255.255.255.0
 default-router 10.20.1.1
 dns-server 10.20.1.10

check.statement: Fill in the blank: the four-step DHCP exchange is often remembered by the acronym D-O-R-A, standing for Discover, Offer, Request, and ____.

solution.explanation: DORA = Discover (client asks), Offer (server proposes an address), Request (client asks to use it), Acknowledge (server confirms the lease).
